However, achieving high visual quality still requires neural networks that are costly to train and render, while recent faster methods inevitably trade off speed for quality. For unbounded and complete scenes (rather than isolated objects) and 1080p resolution rendering, no current method can achieve real-time display rates. We introduce three key elements that allow us to achieve state-of-the-art visual quality while maintaining competitive training times and importantly allow high-quality real-time (≥ 30 fps) novel-view synthesis at 1080p resolution. First, starting from sparse points produced during camera calibration, we represent the scene with 3D Gaussians that preserve desirable properties of continuous volumetric radiance fields for scene optimization while avoiding unnecessary computation in empty space; Second, we perform interleaved optimization/density control of the 3D Gaussians, notably optimizing anisotropic covariance to achieve an accurate representation of the scene; Third, we develop a fast visibility-aware rendering algorithm that supports anisotropic splatting and both accelerates training and allows real-time rendering. We demonstrate state-of-the-art visual quality and real-time rendering on several established datasets.\n\n## Control4D: Efficient 4D Portrait Editing with Text (2023)\n\nRuizhi Shao, Jingxiang Sun, Cheng Peng, Zerong Zheng, Boyao Zhou, Hongwen Zhang, Yebin Liu\n\nDynamic Editing Project\n\n[📄 Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2305.20082.pdf) [🌐 Project](https://control4darxiv.github.io/)📖 Show Abstract\n\nWe introduce Control4D, an innovative framework for editing dynamic 4D portraits using text instructions. Our method addresses the prevalent challenges in 4D editing, notably the inefficiencies of existing 4D representations and the inconsistent editing effect caused by diffusion-based editors. We first propose GaussianPlanes, a novel 4D representation that makes Gaussian Splatting more structured by applying plane-based decomposition in 3D space and time. This enhances both efficiency and robustness in 4D editing. Furthermore, we propose to leverage a 4D generator to learn a more continuous generation space from inconsistent edited images produced by the diffusion-based editor, which effectively improves the consistency and quality of 4D editing. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates the superiority of Control4D, including significantly reduced training time, high-quality rendering, and spatial-temporal consistency in 4D portrait editing.\n\n## Align Your Gaussians: Text-to-4D with Dynamic 3D Gaussians and Composed Diffusion Models (2023)\n\nAndreas Blattmann, Robin Rombach, Huan Ling, Tim Dockhorn, Seung Wook Kim, Sanja Fidler, Karsten Kreis\n\nDiffusion Project\n\n[📄 Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.08818.pdf) [🌐 Project](https://research.nvidia.com/labs/toronto-ai/AlignYourGaussians/)📖 Show Abstract\n\nRecent advancements in 3D reconstruction from single images have been driven by the evolution of generative models. Prominent among these are methods based on Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) and the adaptation of diffusion models in the 3D domain. Despite their progress, these techniques often face limitations due to slow optimization or rendering processes, leading to extensive training and optimization times. In this paper, we introduce a novel approach for single-view reconstruction that efficiently generates a 3D model from a single image via feed-forward inference. Our method utilizes two transformer-based networks, namely a point decoder and a triplane decoder, to reconstruct 3D objects using a hybrid Triplane-Gaussian intermediate representation. This hybrid representation strikes a balance, achieving a faster rendering speed compared to implicit representations while simultaneously delivering superior rendering quality than explicit representations. The point decoder is designed for generating point clouds from single images, offering an explicit representation which is then utilized by the triplane decoder to query Gaussian features for each point. This design choice addresses the challenges associated with directly regressing explicit 3D Gaussian attributes characterized by their non-structural nature. Subsequently, the 3D Gaussians are decoded by an MLP to enable rapid rendering through splatting. Both decoders are built upon a scalable, transformer-based architecture and have been efficiently trained on large-scale 3D datasets. The evaluations conducted on both synthetic datasets and real-world images demonstrate that our method not only achieves higher quality but also ensures a faster runtime in comparison to previous state-of-the-art techniques.\n\n",
  "url": "https://mrnerf.github.io/awesome-3D-gaussian-splatting/"
}
```

{
  "markdown": "\n A Collection of Video Generation Studies \n\nThis GitHub repository summarizes papers and resources related to the video generation task. \n\nIf you have any suggestions about this repository, please feel free to [start a new issue](https://github.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/awesome-video-generation/issues/new) or [pull requests](https://github.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/awesome-video-generation/pulls).\n\nRecent news of this GitHub repo are listed as follows.\n\n🔥 [Dec. 11th, 2025] Our paper titled [\"StableV2V: Stablizing Shape Consistency in Video-to-Video Editing\"](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/11272911) is accepted at TCSVT 2025!\n\n🔥 [Nov. 19th] We have released our latest paper titled [\"StableV2V: Stablizing Shape Consistency in Video-to-Video Editing\"](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.11045), with the correponding [code](https://github.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/StableV2V), [model weights](https://huggingface.co/AlonzoLeeeooo/StableV2V), and [a testing benchmark &#96;DAVIS-Edit&#96;](https://huggingface.co/datasets/AlonzoLeeeooo/DAVIS-Edit) open-sourced. Feel free to check them out from the links!\n Click to see more information. \n\n- [2025 May 13th] Update a new sub-task named [Human Image Animation](#human-image-animation). All **CVPR 2025** papers and references are updated.\n- [Jun. 17th] All **NeurIPS 2023** papers and references are updated.\n- [Apr. 26th] Update a new direction: [Personalized Video Generation](#personalized-video-generation).\n- [Mar. 28th] The official **AAAI 2024** paper list are released! [[Code]](https://github.com/google-research/google-research/blob/master/frechet_video_distance/frechet_video_distance.py)\n\n \n# Q\u0026A\n- **Q: The conference sequence of this paper list?**\n - This paper list is organized according to the following sequence:\n - CVPR\n - ICCV\n - ECCV\n - NeurIPS\n - ICLR\n - AAAI\n - ACM MM\n - SIGGRAPH\n - arXiv\n - Others\n- **Q: What does &#96;Others&#96; refers to?**\n - Some of the following studies (e.g., &#96;Sora&#96;) does not publish their technical report on arXiv. Instead, they tend to write a blog in their official websites. The &#96;Others&#96; category refers to such kind of studies.\n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n\n \n# References\n\nThe &#96;reference.bib&#96; file summarizes bibtex references of up-to-date image inpainting papers, widely used datasets, and toolkits.\nBased on the original references, I have made the following modifications to make their results look nice in the &#96;LaTeX&#96; manuscripts:\n- Refereces are normally constructed in the form of &#96;author-etal-year-nickname&#96;. Particularly, references of datasets and toolkits are directly constructed as &#96;nickname&#96;, e.g., &#96;imagenet&#96;.\n- In each reference, all names of conferences/journals are converted into abbreviations, e.g., &#96;Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition -\u003e CVPR&#96;.\n- The &#96;url&#96;, &#96;doi&#96;, &#96;publisher&#96;, &#96;organization&#96;, &#96;editor&#96;, &#96;series&#96; in all references are removed.\n- The &#96;pages&#96; of all references are added if they are missing.\n- All paper names are in title case. Besides, I have added an additional &#96;{}&#96; to make sure that the title case would also work well in some particular templates. \n\nIf you have other demands of reference formats, you may refer to the original references of papers by searching their names in [DBLP](https://dblp.org/) or [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/).\n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n\n \n# Star History\n\n \n \n \n \n \n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n\n \n# WeChat Group\n\n \n \n \n \n \n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/awesome-video-generation/main/README.md"
}
```

Latents](https://cdn.openai.com/papers/dall-e-2.pdf)*\n--\u003e\n\n## ⭐ Citation\n\nIf you find this paper and repo helpful for your research, please cite it Other Related Works](#head6)\n * - [ ] [📝Prompt Engineering📝](#head-pe)\n * - [ ] [⭐Multimodality⭐](#head-mm)\n * - [ ] [🛫Applications🛫](#head-app)\n * - [ ] [Text+Image/Video → Image/Video](#head-ti2i)\n * - [ ] [Audio+Text+Image/Video → Image/Video](#head-ati2i)\n * - [ ] [Layout/Mask → Image](#head-l2i)\n * - [ ] [Label-set → Semantic maps](#head-l2s)\n * - [ ] [Speech → Image](#head-s2i)\n * - [ ] [Scene Graph → Image](#head-sg2i)\n * - [ ] [Text → Visual Retrieval](#head-t2vr)\n * - [ ] [Text → 3D/Motion/Shape/Mesh/Object...](#head-t2m)\n * - [ ] [Text → Video](#head-t2v)\n * - [ ] [Text → Music](#head-t2music)\n\n* [*Contact Me*](#head7)\n\n ## *1. Description* \n\n* In the last few decades, the fields of Computer Vision (CV) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) have been made several major technological breakthroughs in deep learning research. Recently, researchers appear interested in combining semantic information and visual information in these traditionally independent fields. \nA number of studies have been conducted on text-to-image synthesis techniques that transfer input textual descriptions (keywords or sentences) into realistic images.\n\n* Papers, codes, and datasets for the text-to-image task are available here.\n\n\u003e🐌 Markdown Format:\n\u003e * (Conference/Journal Year) **Title**, First Author et al. [[Paper](URL)] [[Code](URL)] [[Project](URL)]\n\n ## *2. Quantitative Evaluation Metrics* [ «🎯Back To Top» ](#)\n\n* Inception Score (IS) [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1606.03498.pdf)] [[Python Code (Pytorch)](https://github.com/sbarratt/inception-score-pytorch)] [(New!)[Python Code (Tensorflow)](https://github.com/senmaoy/Inception-Score-FID-on-CUB-and-OXford)] [[Python Code (Tensorflow)](https://github.com/taki0112/GAN_Metrics-Tensorflow)] [[Ref.Code(AttnGAN)](https://github.com/taoxugit/AttnGAN)]\n\n* Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) [[Paper](https://papers.nips.cc/paper/7240-gans-trained-by-a-two-time-scale-update-rule-converge-to-a-local-nash-equilibrium.pdf)] [[Python Code (Pytorch)](https://github.com/mseitzer/pytorch-fid)] [(New!)[Python Code (Tensorflow)](https://github.com/senmaoy/Inception-Score-FID-on-CUB-and-OXford)] [[Python Code (Tensorflow)](https://github.com/taki0112/GAN_Metrics-Tensorflow)] [[Ref.Code(DM-GAN)](https://github.com/MinfengZhu/DM-GAN)]\n\n* R-precision [[Paper](https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content_cvpr_2018/papers/Xu_AttnGAN_Fine-Grained_Text_CVPR_2018_paper.pdf)] [[Ref.Code(CPGAN)](https://github.com/dongdongdong666/CPGAN)]\n\n* L 2 error [[Paper](https://papers.nips.cc/paper/7290-text-adaptive-generative-adversarial-networks-manipulating-images-with-natural-language.pdf)]\n\n* Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS) [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/1801.03924)] [[Python Code](https://github.com/richzhang/PerceptualSimilarity)]\n\n* CLIPScore [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.08718)] [[Python Code](https://github.com/jmhessel/clipscore)]\n\n* MID [[Paper](https://openreview.net/forum?id=wKd2XtSRsjl)] [[Python Code](https://github.com/naver-ai/mid.metric)]\n\n* PickScore [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.01569)] [[Python Code](https://huggingface.co/yuvalkirstain/PickScore_v1)]\n\n* Human Preference Score v2 (HPS v2) [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.09341)] [[Python Code](https://github.com/tgxs002/HPSv2)]\n\n## *3. Datasets* [ «🎯Back To Top» ](#)\n \n* **Oxford-102 Flower** \n\n Oxford-102 Flower is a 102-category dataset, consisting of 102 flower categories. The flowers are chosen to be flowers commonly occurring in the United Kingdom. The images have large scale, pose and light variations. \n * **Detailed information (Images):** ⇒ [[Paper](http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/publications/2008/Nilsback08/nilsback08.pdf)] [[Website](http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/flowers/102/index.html)]\n * Number of different categories: 102 (**Training**: 82 categories. **Testing**: 20 categories.)\n * Number of flower images: 8,189\n * **Detailed information (Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content_cvpr_2016/papers/Reed_Learning_Deep_Representations_CVPR_2016_paper.pdf)] [[Download](https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B0ywwgffWnLLcms2WWJQRFNSWXM/view?usp=sharing\u0026resourcekey=0-Av8zFbeDDvNcF1sSjDR32w)]\n * Descriptions per image: 10 Captions\n \n* **Caltech-UCSD Bird(CUB)** \n\n Caltech-UCSD Birds-200-2011 (CUB-200-2011) is an extended version of the CUB-200 dataset, with roughly double the number of images per class and new part location annotations.\n * **Detailed information (Images):** ⇒ [[Paper](http://www.vision.caltech.edu/visipedia/papers/CUB_200_2011.pdf)] [[Website](http://www.vision.caltech.edu/datasets/cub_200_2011/)]\n * Number of different categories: 200 (**Training**: 150 categories. **Testing**: 50 categories.)\n * Number of bird images: 11,788\n * Annotations per image: 15 Part Locations, 312 Binary Attributes, 1 Bounding Box, Ground-truth Segmentation\n * **Detailed information (Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content_cvpr_2016/papers/Reed_Learning_Deep_Representations_CVPR_2016_paper.pdf)] [[Website](https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B0ywwgffWnLLZW9uVHNjb2JmNlE/view)]\n * Descriptions per image: 10 Captions\n\n* **MS-COCO** \n\n COCO is a large-scale object detection, segmentation, and captioning dataset.\n * **Detailed information (Images):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1405.0312.pdf)] [[Website](https://cocodataset.org/#overview)]\n * Number of different categories: 91\n * Number of images: 120k (**Training**: 80k. **Testing**: 40k.)\n * **Detailed information (Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1405.0312.pdf)] [[Download](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1GOEl9lxgSsWUWOXkZZrch08GgPADze7U/view?usp=sharing)]\n * Descriptions per image: 5 Captions\n \n* **Multi-Modal-CelebA-HQ** \n\n Multi-Modal-CelebA-HQ is a large-scale face image dataset for text-to-image-generation, text-guided image manipulation, sketch-to-image generation, GANs for face generation and editing, image caption, and VQA.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2012.03308.pdf)] [[Website](https://github.com/weihaox/Multi-Modal-CelebA-HQ-Dataset)] [[Download](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1eVrGKfkbw7bh9xPcX8HJa-qWQTD9aWvf)]\n * Number of images (from Celeba-HQ): 30,000 (**Training**: 24,000. **Testing**: 6,000.)\n * Descriptions per image: 10 Captions\n * **Detailed information (Masks):** \n * Number of masks (from Celeba-Mask-HQ): 30,000 (512 x 512)\n * **Detailed information (Sketches):** \n * Number of Sketches: 30,000 (512 x 512)\n * **Detailed information (Image with transparent background):** \n * Not fully uploaded\n\n* **CelebA-Dialog** \n\n CelebA-Dialog is a large-scale visual-language face dataset. It has two properties:\n(1) Facial images are annotated with **rich fine-grained labels**, which classify one attribute into multiple degrees according to its semantic meaning.\n(2) Accompanied with each image, there are **captions describing** the attributes and a **user request sample**.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content/ICCV2021/papers/Jiang_Talk-To-Edit_Fine-Grained_Facial_Editing_via_Dialog_ICCV_2021_paper.pdf)] [[Website](http://mmlab.ie.cuhk.edu.hk/projects/CelebA/CelebA_Dialog.html)] [[Download](https://github.com/yumingj/Talk-to-Edit)]\n * Number of identities: 10,177\n * Number of images: 202,599 \n * 5 fine-grained attributes annotations per image: Bangs, Eyeglasses, Beard, Smiling, and Age\n\n\n* **FFHQ-Text** \n\n FFHQ-Text is a small-scale face image dataset with large-scale facial attributes, designed for text-to-face generation \u0026 manipulation, text-guided facial image manipulation, and other vision-related tasks.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.1145/3474085.3481026)] [[Website](https://github.com/Yutong-Zhou-cv/FFHQ-Text_Dataset)] [[Download](https://forms.gle/f7oMXD3g9BgdgEUd7)]\n * Number of images (from FFHQ): 760 (**Training**: 500. **Testing**: 260.)\n * Descriptions per image: 9 Captions\n * 13 multi-valued facial element groups from coarse to fine.\n * **Detailed information (BBox):** ⇒ [[Website](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/software/via/)]\n\n* **CelebAText-HQ** \n\n CelebAText-HQ is a large-scale face image dataset with large-scale facial attributes, designed for text-to-face generation.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.1145/3474085.3475391)] [[Website](https://github.com/cripac-sjx/SEA-T2F)] [[Download](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1IAb_iy6-soEGQWhbgu6cQODsIUJZpahC)]\n * Number of images (from Celeba-HQ): 15010 (**Training**: 13,710. **Testing**: 1300.)\n * Descriptions per image: 10 Captions\n\n* **DeepFashion-MultiModal** \n \n DeepFashion-MultiModal is a large-scale high-quality human dataset. Human images are annotated with **rich multi-modal labels**, including human parsing labels, keypoints, densepose, fine-grained attributes and textual descriptions.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2205.15996.pdf)] [[Website](https://github.com/yumingj/DeepFashion-MultiModal)] [[Download](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1An2c_ZCkeGmhJg0zUjtZF46vyJgQwIr2?usp=sharing)]\n * Number of images: 44,096, including 12,701 full-body images\n * Descriptions per image: 1 Caption\n\n* **ANNA** \n\n ANNA is an Abstractive News captioNs dAtaset extracted from online news articles in a variety of different contexts. The generated images are judged on the basis of contextual relevance, visual quality, and perceptual similarity to ground-truth image-caption pairs.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.02160)] [[Download](https://github.com/aashish2000/ANNA)]\n * Number of image-text pairs (from The New York Times): 29625 (**Training**: 17897. **Validation**: 1622. **Testing**: 1649.)\n\n* **Bento800-Text** \n\n Bento800 is the first manually annotated synthetic box lunch presentation dataset with diverse annotations(BBox, Segmentation, Labels... ) for novel aesthetic box lunch presentation design.\n * **Detailed information (Images \u0026 Text Descriptions):** ⇒ [[Paper](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3552485.3554935)] [[Website](https://github.com/Yutong-Zhou-cv/Bento800_Dataset)] [[Download](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1_VvAbIzeuVew4fa98CcE11mB9SoAI3q-?usp=sharing)]\n * Number of images: 800 (**Training**: 766. **Testing**: 34.)\n * Descriptions per image: 9 Captions\n\n* **Others** \n * (arXiv preprint 2023) **AGIQA-3K** [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.04717)] [[Dataset](https://github.com/lcysyzxdxc/AGIQA-3k-Database/tree/main)]\n * (CVPR 2023) [💬 Video] **CelebV-Text** [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.14717)] [[Github](https://github.com/celebv-text/CelebV-Text)] [[Project](https://celebv-text.github.io/)]\n * (ECCV 2022) [💬 Video] **CelebV-HQ** [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.12393)] [[Github](https://github.com/celebv-hq/CelebV-HQ)] [[Project](https://celebv-hq.github.io/)]\n\n\n## *4. Project* **[ «🎯Back To Top» ](#)**\n* ⭐⭐**Stable Cascade**. [[Website](https://stability.ai/news/introducing-stable-cascade)] [[Github](https://github.com/Stability-AI/StableCascade)] [[Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/stable-cascade)] [[Unofficial Demo](https://huggingface.co/spaces/multimodalart/stable-cascade)]\n * \u003e A new text-to-image model building upon the [Würstchen](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=gU58d5QeGv) architecture. \n * \u003e This model is being released under a non-commercial license that permits non-commercial use only.\n * \u003e Stable Cascade is exceptionally easy to train and finetune on consumer hardware thanks to its three-stage approach.\n* **Hotpot-AI Art Generator**. [[Project](https://hotpot.ai/art-generator?s=stable-diffusion#)] \n * \u003e Our AI image generator brings imagination to life, producing stunning art, illustrations, and photos in seconds. Unleash creativity and express yourself in new ways with the power of AI. \n* **Generative Fill**. [[Project](https://www.adobe.com/products/photoshop/generative-fill.html)] \n * \u003e Add, remove, or expand content in images right in Adobe Photoshop with simple text prompts powered by Adobe Firefly generative AI.\n* **LLaVA-Interactive**. [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.00571v1)] [[Project](https://llava-vl.github.io/llava-interactive/)] [[Demo](https://llavainteractive.ngrok.io/)] \n * \u003e Image Chat, Segmentation, and Generation/Editing -- All-in-one demo\n* **Mini-DALL•E 3**. [[Demo](http://139.224.23.16:10085/)] \n * \u003e Interactive text to image (iT2I)\n* **Stable Doodle**. [[ClipDrop](https://clipdrop.co/stable-doodle)] \n * \u003e Transform your doodles into real images in seconds.\n* **StableStudio**. [[Github](https://github.com/Stability-AI/StableStudio)] \n * \u003e The open-source version of [DreamStudio](https://dreamstudio.ai/).\n* ⭐**Stable Diffusion XL**. [[ClipDrop](https://clipdrop.co/stable-diffusion)] [[Website](https://stability.ai/blog/stable-diffusion-xl-beta-available-for-api-customers-and-dreamstudio-users)] [[DreamStudio Beta](https://beta.dreamstudio.ai/dream)]\n * \u003e Next-level photorealism capabilities; Enhanced image composition and face generation; Rich visuals and jaw-dropping aesthetics; Use of shorter prompts to create descriptive imagery; Greater capability to produce legible text.\n * \u003e SDXL also has functionality that extends beyond just text-to-image prompting, including ***image-to-image prompting*** (inputting one image to get variations of that image), ***inpainting*** (reconstructing missing parts of an image) and ***outpainting*** (constructing a seamless extension of an existing image). \n* ⭐**Image2Paragraph**. [[Github](https://github.com/showlab/Image2Paragraph)] [[Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/spaces/Awiny/Image2Paragraph)]\n * From ***One-to-many*** to ***One-to-one***\n* **2022 Top Papers in AI — A Year of Generative Models**. [[Medium](https://chuanenlin.medium.com/2022-top-ai-papers-a-year-of-generative-models-a7dcd9109e39)] \n* **Whisper-to-stable-diffusion**. [[Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/spaces/fffiloni/whisper-to-stable-diffusion)] \n * \u003e***Whisper*** is a general-purpose speech recognition model. It is trained on a large dataset of diverse audio. It is also a multi-task model that can perform multilingual speech recognition as well as speech translation and language identification.\n * \u003e***Stable Diffusion*** is a state-of-the-art text-to-image model that generates images from text.\n* ⭐⭐**Stable Diffusion 2.0**. [[Github](https://github.com/Stability-AI/stablediffusion)] [[Website](https://stability.ai/blog/stable-diffusion-v2-release)] [[Stability AI API Platform](https://platform.stability.ai/)] [[DreamStudio Beta](https://beta.dreamstudio.ai/dream)]\n * \u003e***New Text-to-Image Diffusion Model:*** Generates images with default resolutions of both 512x512 pixels and 768x768 pixels and remove adult content using LAION’s NSFW filter.\n * \u003e***Super-resolution Upscaler Diffusion Model:*** Enhances the resolution of images by a factor of 4.\n * \u003e***Depth-to-Image Diffusion Model:*** Depth2img infers the depth of an input image (using [an existing model](https://github.com/isl-org/MiDaS)), and then generates new images using both the text and depth information. New images look radically different from the original but still preserve the coherence and depth of that image.\n * \u003e***Updated Inpainting Diffusion Model:*** A new text-guided inpainting model, fine-tuned on the new Stable Diffusion 2.0 base text-to-image, which makes it super easy to switch out parts of an image intelligently and quickly.\n \n* ⭐**Stable Diffusion**. [[**Awesome Stable-Diffusion**](https://github.com/awesome-stable-diffusion/awesome-stable-diffusion)] [[Researcher Access Form](https://stability.ai/research-access-form)] [[Github](https://github.com/CompVis/stable-diffusion)] [[Web UI](https://github.com/sd-webui/stable-diffusion-webui)] [[WebUI Docker](https://github.com/AbdBarho/stable-diffusion-webui-docker)] [[Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/spaces/stabilityai/stable-diffusion)] [[DreamStudio Beta](https://beta.dreamstudio.ai/dream)]\n * \u003eStable Diffusion is a text-to-image model that will empower billions of people to create stunning art within seconds. It is a breakthrough in speed and quality meaning that it can run on consumer GPUs.\n* **Midjourney**. [[Documentation](https://midjourney.gitbook.io/docs/)] [[Homepage](https://www.midjourney.com/home/)] \n * \u003eMidjourney is an independent research lab exploring new mediums of thought and expanding the imaginative powers of the human species.\nThere are two ways to experience the tools: the Midjourney Bot and the [Web App](https://www.midjourney.com/).\n* **Artflowq**. [[Start Creating!](https://artflow.ai/)] \n * \u003eArtflow lets users generate visual content with the help of AI. Create unique avatars with ease and turn any description into a portrait.\n* **craiyon(~~DALL·E Mini~~)**. [[Short Video Explanation](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qOxde_JV0vI)] [[Blog](https://www.louisbouchard.ai/dalle-mini/)] [[Github](https://github.com/borisdayma/dalle-mini)] [[Huggingface official demo](https://huggingface.co/spaces/dalle-mini/dalle-mini)] [[Homepage](https://www.craiyon.com/)] [[min(DALL·E)](https://github.com/kuprel/min-dalle)]\n * \u003eA free, open-source AI that produces amazing images from text inputs. AI model drawing images from any prompt!\n* **Disco Diffusion**. [[Github](https://github.com/alembics/disco-diffusion)] [[Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/alembics/disco-diffusion/blob/main/Disco_Diffusion.ipynb)]\n * \u003eA Frankensteinian amalgamation of notebooks, models and techniques for the generation of AI Art and Animations.\n* **Aphantasia**. [[Github](https://github.com/eps696/aphantasia)] \n * \u003eThis is a text-to-image tool, part of the [artwork](https://computervisionart.com/pieces2021/aphantasia/) of the same name. ([Aphantasia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aphantasia) is the inability to visualize mental images, the deprivation of visual dreams.)\n* **Text2Art**. [[Try it now!](https://text2art.com/)] [[Github](https://github.com/mfrashad/text2art)] [[Blog](https://towardsdatascience.com/how-i-built-an-ai-text-to-art-generator-a0c0f6d6f59f)] \n * \u003eText2Art is an AI-powered art generator based on VQGAN+CLIP that can generate all kinds of art such as pixel art, drawing, and painting from just text input. \n* **Survey Text Based Image Synthesis** [[Blog](https://hackmd.io/@prajwalsingh/imagesynthesis#) (2021)]\n\n## *5. {
  "markdown": "\n A Collection of Video Generation Studies \n\nThis GitHub repository summarizes papers and resources related to the video generation task. \n\nIf you have any suggestions about this repository, please feel free to [start a new issue](https://github.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/awesome-video-generation/issues/new) or [pull requests](https://github.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/awesome-video-generation/pulls).\n\nRecent news of this GitHub repo are listed as follows.\n\n🔥 [Dec. 11th, 2025] Our paper titled [\"StableV2V: Stablizing Shape Consistency in Video-to-Video Editing\"](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/11272911) is accepted at TCSVT 2025!\n\n🔥 [Nov. 19th] We have released our latest paper titled [\"StableV2V: Stablizing Shape Consistency in Video-to-Video Editing\"](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.11045), with the correponding [code](https://github.com/AlonzoLeeeooo/StableV2V), [model weights](https://huggingface.co/AlonzoLeeeooo/StableV2V), and [a testing benchmark &#96;DAVIS-Edit&#96;](https://huggingface.co/datasets/AlonzoLeeeooo/DAVIS-Edit) open-sourced. Feel free to check them out from the links!\n Click to see more information. \n\n- [2025 May 13th] Update a new sub-task named [Human Image Animation](#human-image-animation). All **CVPR 2025** papers and references are updated.\n- [Jun. 17th] All **NeurIPS 2023** papers and references are updated.\n- [Apr. 26th] Update a new direction: [Personalized Video Generation](#personalized-video-generation).\n- [Mar. 28th] The official **AAAI 2024** paper list are released! This paper list is organized according to the following sequence:\n - CVPR\n - ICCV\n - ECCV\n - NeurIPS\n - ICLR\n - AAAI\n - ACM MM\n - SIGGRAPH\n - arXiv\n - Others\n- **Q: What does &#96;Others&#96; refers to?**\n - Some of the following studies (e.g., &#96;Sora&#96;) does not publish their technical report on arXiv. Instead, they tend to write a blog in their official websites. The &#96;Others&#96; category refers to such kind of studies.\n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n\n \n# References\n\nThe &#96;reference.bib&#96; file summarizes bibtex references of up-to-date image inpainting papers, widely used datasets, and toolkits.\nBased on the original references, I have made the following modifications to make their results look nice in the &#96;LaTeX&#96; manuscripts:\n- Refereces are normally constructed in the form of &#96;author-etal-year-nickname&#96;. Particularly, references of datasets and toolkits are directly constructed as &#96;nickname&#96;, e.g., &#96;imagenet&#96;.\n- In each reference, all names of conferences/journals are converted into abbreviations, e.g., &#96;Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition -\u003e CVPR&#96;.\n- The &#96;url&#96;, &#96;doi&#96;, &#96;publisher&#96;, &#96;organization&#96;, &#96;editor&#96;, &#96;series&#96; in all references are removed.\n- The &#96;pages&#96; of all references are added if they are missing.\n- All paper names are in title case. Besides, I have added an additional &#96;{}&#96; to make sure that the title case would also work well in some particular templates. \n\nIf you have other demands of reference formats, you may refer to the original references of papers by searching their names in [DBLP](https://dblp.org/) or [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/).\n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n\n \n# Star History\n\n \n \n \n \n \n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n\n \n# WeChat Group\n\n \n \n \n \n \n\n[ \u003c🎯Back to Top\u003e](#contents)\n",

}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Creating a summary for video generation**

I need to craft a summary for video generation, organizing it by sections with year subsections. The 3DGS website has filters by year and categories, and I should confirm these categories, possibly using tags like "Reconstruction" or "Compression."

I'll focus on tagging sections with a year filter, placing each entry in chronological order. For relighting, I’ll create a straightforward chronological list, while for sketches, I’ll organize them by application direction. The text-to-image section seems a bit mixed with various checklists.
**Structuring a topic-based summary**

I’m focusing on creating a summary that's both topic-based and chronological. The main list will organize sections by topics like Survey, Datasets, and Projects, while each section might have year headings.

For the visualization repository, I need to use research directions for primary navigation and add yearly subsections for the last 2-3 years or keep them chronologically tagged. _**Agent (default)**_

### 这 5 个综述 repo 是怎么组织“最新论文”的？

- **`awesome-video-generation`**
  - **主组织方式**：先按**研究子任务/方向**（Text-to-Video、Image-to-Video、Video Editing…）分大类
  - **“最新论文”落地方式**：每个方向下面再按**年份（Year 2026/2025/…）**分块；同时在最上方维护一个 **Recent news / 更新日志 / To-Do（按会议年份补齐）**来保证“最新”可见、可追踪

- **`awesome-3D-gaussian-splatting`（网页型）**
  - **主组织方式**：网页提供**按年份筛选/跳转**（例如 All Years / 2025 / 2024 / 2023），每条工作本身标题里也带年份
  - **“最新论文”落地方式**：天然偏**时间线/年度聚合**（更像“持续滚动更新的列表”），并通过网页交互降低检索成本

- **`Awesome-Relighting`**
  - **主组织方式**：非常明确的**按时间（YYYY.MM）倒序/顺序**滚动维护（先 Survey，再 Papers）
  - **“最新论文”落地方式**：靠**月份粒度**把最新工作直接顶在前面（或持续追加），不太强调按方向分组

- **`Awesome-Sketch-Based-Applications`**
  - **主组织方式**：先按**应用/方向**分章节（1. Sketch Based Image Synthesis、2. Sketch Based Image Editing…），每章用表格列论文
  - **“最新论文”落地方式**：主要通过**方向章节内追加新行**；年份信息通常体现在 `Source`（会议/期刊 + 年份），但不是“按年份分段”

- **`Awesome-Text-to-Image`**
  - **主组织方式**：明显是**混合型**：有 **News/Recently Focused**（强调最新），正文既有**按主题块**，也预留/包含**按年份（2024/2023/…）**的入口
  - **“最新论文”落地方式**：用 **News + Recently Focused** 保证“最新”可见，同时正文可按主题或时间查

**一句话总结**：这 5 个里，最常见的最佳实践是 **“按方向组织正文” + “用年份（或时间）保证最新可追踪”**；纯按年份滚动（Relighting）适合主题非常聚焦、边界清晰的领域。

---

### 你当前 `awesome-visualization` 应该按年份加，还是按研究方向加？

对 Visualization 这种大领域，我建议你采用：

- **主线（README 正文）按研究方向添加**：你现在的 `README.md` 已经是这种结构（按方向分大类），这会让读者“先定位子领域，再找论文”最省力。
- **每个方向内部再轻量引入年份**：当某个方向条目开始变多时，把该方向下拆成 `2026 / 2025 / 2024 / Classic`（或只拆近 2-3 年 + Classic）。这样你既能维持方向清晰，又能自然突出最新。
- **额外加一块“Recent / 本月新增”**（可选但强烈推荐）：在 README 顶部或单独章节列出最近新增的 10–30 条，解决“最新论文在哪”的需求（这是 video-generation / text-to-image 的强项）。

---

### 你具体该怎么往当前 repo 里加内容（最实用的操作方式）

- **新增一篇论文时**：先判断它属于哪个方向（Interaction / Graph / SciVis / Geo / Evaluation…）→ 直接加到对应小节里，按你 README 约定格式写条目。
- **如果你想强调“最新”**：同一条目可以同时出现在
  - **方向小节**（作为长期归档，主位置）
  - **Recent 小节**（作为短期展示，可定期滚动移除旧条目）
- **当某方向超过 ~30–50 篇**：再在该方向下按年份拆分（这时再拆最省维护成本，也更自然）。

如果你愿意，我可以直接在你的 `README.md` 里加一个很克制的 `## Recent Updates` 区块模板（不改变你现有结构），让你从第一天开始就能“滚动更新最新论文”。

### 你的 `README.md` 组织架构是否“正确”？

**是正确的，而且方向是对的**：对 Visualization 这种大领域，“先按研究方向（topic）组织正文，再用年份/Recent 体现最新”通常是最可维护、对读者也最友好的结构。你现在的 README 已经具备这套骨架（目录 → 贡献方式 → 条目格式 → 主题总览 → 按方向论文 → 数据集/工具/课程等）。

你现在的核心结构在这里：

```20:44:/Users/yutiaoyi/developer/awesome-visualization/README.md
## 目录

- [更新日志](#更新日志)
- [如何贡献](#如何贡献)
- [阅读方式与条目格式](#阅读方式与条目格式)
- [主题总览（建议分类）](#主题总览建议分类)
- [精选综述 / 教程](#精选综述--教程)
- [论文（按方向）](#论文按方向)
  - [可视化基础与设计理论](#可视化基础与设计理论)
  - [可视分析与交互](#可视分析与交互)
  - [可解释性与模型可视化](#可解释性与模型可视化)
  - [图可视化 / 网络可视化](#图可视化--网络可视化)
  - [时序与事件序列可视化](#时序与事件序列可视化)
  - [高维数据可视化与降维](#高维数据可视化与降维)
  - [不确定性可视化](#不确定性可视化)
  - [科学可视化（体渲染/流场/医学等）](#科学可视化体渲染流场医学等)
  - [地理空间与时空可视化](#地理空间与时空可视化)
  - [文本/多模态可视化](#文本多模态可视化)
  - [评测、基准与复现](#评测基准与复现)
```

**唯一我会强调的点**：这套结构“正确”不代表它是唯一标准；Awesome 列表更像信息架构（IA），目标是让你的目标读者最快找到东西、让你最省力维护。你当前的骨架已经达标。

---

### 你的 Visualization 分类是否“对”？真的能分成这几类吗？

**可以分成你现在这几类**，因为它们覆盖了 VIS 领域最常见的“检索维度”：  
- **按数据类型/问题形态**：图（Graph）、时序（Time）、高维（High-D）、地理（Geo）、文本（Text）  
- **按方法/研究关注点**：交互与VA、设计与理论、不确定性、评测  
- **按分支传统**：SciVis（体渲染/流场/医学等）

但也要承认：Visualization 的边界天然“交叉”，不存在一个全社区统一、完全无重叠的划分。你现在的分类是**合理且常用的一个切法**，适合作为 repo 的第一版。

---

### 我建议你做的两点“微调”（让分类更像 VIS 社区常用口径）

- **建议 1：把“可解释性与模型可视化”改成更中性的上位类**
  - 现在这类更像近年热点（XAI/ML 可视化），当然可以保留，但建议命名为 **“AI/ML 可视化与可解释性（AI/ML for Vis & Vis for AI）”** 或类似表述，避免给人“Visualization = XAI”为主的错觉。
  - 这样也更容易容纳：ML-driven visualization、可视化推荐、自动标注、LLM+Vis、可视分析中的模型等。

- **建议 2：补一个“可视化系统与工程 / 可视化语法与工具链”（可选）**
  - 你现在有“工具/系统/开源项目”章节，但论文区里缺少一个容器来放：grammar/grammar-of-graphics、可视化编程模型、声明式可视化、系统架构、可扩展渲染与部署、交互框架（研究论文层面的）。
  - 如果你未来会大量收录系统类论文（VIS/CHI/UIST/EuroVis 的系统与语言），加这一类会更顺。

---

### 结论（直接回答你的两个问题）

- **架构是否正确**：**正确**，而且非常适合做“综述型 Awesome repo”的长期维护。
- **分类是否对**：**对，且可行**；Visualization 确实常被按你这种“数据类型 + 方法关注点 + SciVis 分支”的方式组织。后续只需要根据你实际收录的论文分布，做少量合并/拆分即可。

如果你希望我给一个“更贴近 IEEE VIS 官方话语体系”的分类版本，我也可以在不推翻你现有结构的前提下，给出一份**可直接替换目录标题**的建议清单（保持你现在的 11 类数量不变，或者缩到 7–9 类更稳）。

### 1) 这篇 `SimVecVis` 我能看到吗？放哪个分类最好？

我能看到它在 arXiv 页面的标题与摘要（`SimVecVis: A Dataset for Enhancing MLLMs in Visualization Understanding`，[arXiv:2506.21319](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.21319)）。

**放置建议（按你当前 README 的分类）**  
- **主放**：`数据集与基准`（因为它核心贡献是**数据集 + 表示格式 + QA 评测/训练资源**）  
- **交叉引用（可选）**：`文本/多模态可视化`（因为它面向 **MLLM 的可视化理解**，属于 Vis × Multimodal/LLM 交叉）

---

### 2) 这篇论文做了什么？（基于摘要的拆解）

根据摘要，这篇工作主要做了三件事：

- **提出 SimVec 表示**：一种“简化的矢量格式”，把图表元素用结构化字段编码（如 mark 类型、位置、大小），目的是让模型更容易对齐“数据→图形映射”，并可用于从 SimVec 重建图表信息。
- **构建 SimVecVis 数据集**：包含三种对齐信息：  
  - 图表位图（bitmap）  
  - 对应的 SimVec 表示  
  - 数据中心的问答（QA）与解释性 CoT 描述  
- **用它微调 MLLM 并验证收益**：在数据中心 QA 任务上提升了部分 MLLM（摘要里点名 MiniCPM、Qwen-VL），尤其是空间感知更强的模型。

一句话：**它是在做“面向图表/可视化理解的结构化中间表示 + 数据集 + 微调验证”。**

---

### 3) 你现在 repo 的“一篇论文条目”由哪些部分组成？

你在 `README.md` 里定义的是这种统一格式（本质上是 Awesome list 的标准做法）：

- **Year/venue**：会议/期刊 + 年份  
- **Title**：标题（加粗）  
- **Authors（可选但推荐）**：`First Author et al.`  
- **Links**：`Paper / Project / Code`（按可得性选填）  
- **Tags**：用于检索的关键词（2–5 个）

也就是你说的：year、title、paper 链接、tag ——对的；另外你还留了 project/code 的位置，这很实用。

---

### 4) 你给的 5 个 repo，“一篇论文”各自是怎么组织的？

- **`awesome-video-generation`**  
  - **位置**：先按子方向，再按 `Year 20xx` 分块  
  - **条目形态**：在年份块下，常见是“先写会议（如 CVPR）再列论文”，每篇通常带 `Paper/Project/Code` 等链接，强调“最新会议年度的补齐与维护”。

- **`awesome-3D-gaussian-splatting`（网页）**  
  - **条目形态**：每篇是一个“卡片/段落”：**标题（含年份）+ 作者 + Paper/Project/Code + 可展开摘要**，并支持按年份筛选。

- **`Awesome-Relighting`**  
  - **条目形态**：强时间线：`[YYYY.MM] [VenueYear] Title [paper] [code/project]`，用月份保证“最新工作”自然靠前。

- **`Awesome-Sketch-Based-Applications`**  
  - **条目形态**：大量用**表格**：`Paper | Source(会议/期刊+年) | Code/Project`；按应用/方向分章节，条目本身不靠年份分段。

- **`Awesome-Text-to-Image`**  
  - **条目形态**：混合但信息密：常见是 `（Venue Year）Title，Authors + Paper/Code/Project/...`；另外还有 `News` / `Recently Focused` 之类的“最新入口”。

### 1) 你这套“信息尽量多”的论文条目格式可以吗？

**可以，而且很推荐**。你列的 5 个字段基本就是 Awesome 类仓库里“信息密度/可检索性/可维护性”的最佳平衡点：

- **Year（年份）**：用于时间线、找最新、做年度索引
- **会议名字（Venue）**：用于判断可信度/领域归属（VIS/TVCG/CHI/UIST/EuroVis…）
- **论文名字（Title）**：最核心的可读信息
- **Paper/Project/Code 链接**：便于快速跟进、复现与落地
- **Tag**：用于跨章节检索与后续聚合（比如同一个 tag 在多个方向里出现）

你可以把它固化成一条统一模板，例如：

```markdown
- (VIS 2025) **Paper Title**，First Author et al. [[Paper]](link) [[Project]](link) [[Code]](link) `Tags: tag1, tag2`
```

（你现在 README 里其实已经非常接近这个格式了。）

---

### 2) 你这个 Markdown 里能做“交叉引用”吗？怎么做？

**完全可以**。Markdown 在 GitHub 上最常用的交叉引用方式有两种：

- **方式 A：锚点链接（推荐，用于同一个 `README.md` 内部）**  
  你只要给“主分类”所在小节一个标题（它本身就会生成锚点），然后在其他分类里用链接指过去。

  例子（假设主条目放在“数据集与基准”小节里）：

```markdown
- (arXiv 2025) **SimVecVis: A Dataset for Enhancing MLLMs in Visualization Understanding** ... `Tags: dataset, chart, mllm`

  - See also: [数据集与基准](#数据集与基准)
```

更细粒度一点的做法：给某一篇论文一个“专用锚点”。做法是在主条目前加一个 HTML anchor（GitHub 支持）：

```markdown
<a id="simvecvis"></a>
- (arXiv 2025) **SimVecVis: A Dataset for Enhancing MLLMs in Visualization Understanding** ... `Tags: dataset, chart, mllm`
```

然后在别的章节写：

```markdown
- (arXiv 2025) **SimVecVis** → 见主条目：[SimVecVis](#simvecvis)
```

- **方式 B：跨文件链接（用于你将来把内容拆到多个 `.md` 文件）**  
  例如主条目在 `lists/datasets.md`，其他地方引用它：

```markdown
- **SimVecVis** → 见：[`lists/datasets.md#simvecvis`](lists/datasets.md#simvecvis)
```

---

如果你想，我可以直接把“交叉引用锚点”的这套写法补充进 `CONTRIBUTING.md` 的规范里，并在 `README.md` 里加一个小的示例（不会破坏你现有结构）。

  - `### 评测、基准与复现` 的表格里也加了一行，`Paper` 列链接到 `#simvecvis`

交叉引用的关键写法就是：
- 在主位置放：`<a id="simvecvis"></a>`
- 在其他位置写链接：`[见下方数据集条目](#simvecvis)`

---

### 你说的“格式不一样/是不是错了”？我已经帮你统一并解释清楚了

你指出的现象是对的：之前
- `### 评测、基准与复现` 还是 **bullet 占位**
- `## 数据集与基准` 变成了 **按年份 + 表格**

这会让人感觉“不一致”。

我现在做了两点修正：
- **格式统一**：把 `文本/多模态可视化`、`评测、基准与复现` 也改成了同样的**表格模板**（含占位行）
- **语义澄清**：在 `## 数据集与基准` 开头加了一段说明，强调：
  - 这里收录可复用的 **datasets/benchmarks 资源**
  - 方法学/复现类论文仍然可以放在上面的 **“评测、基准与复现”**（而不是和数据集混在一起）

如果你希望进一步“彻底不重复”，我也可以下一步把 `## 数据集与基准` 改名成更不歧义的 `## 数据集（Datasets）与 Benchmark Suites`，或者把 “benchmark” 全部只留在一个章节里。

_**Agent (default)**_

### 它们看起来“重复”的原因（以及我当时的目的）

你指出的点是对的：**“评测、基准与复现”** 和 **“数据集与基准”** 在字面上确实容易被理解成同一件事，读者会困惑。

我当初拆成两块的目的其实是把两类东西分开（方便维护、也方便读者找）：

- **评测、基准与复现**：更偏“**论文/方法学**”  
  - 例：评测指标、用户研究方法、复现研究、对比实验协议、错误分析等（不一定有可下载数据集）
- **数据集与基准**：更偏“**可复用资源**”  
  - 例：数据集、benchmark suite（数据+任务+协议+指标，最好带代码/leaderboard）

问题在于：如果都叫“基准”，就会显得重复。

---

### 我已经把 `README.md` 里这两块改到不容易误解的版本

- 把 `### 评测、基准与复现` **改名**为 **`### 评测方法学与复现（Evaluation & Reproducibility）`**，并加了说明：这里放“怎么评测/复现/方法学”的论文。
- 把 `## 数据集与基准` **改名**为 **`## 数据集与 Benchmark Suites`**，并加了说明：这里放可复用的数据与成体系 benchmark 套件。

这样两块的边界会更清晰：**一个偏方法学论文，一个偏可复用资源**。

---

### `SimVecVis` 能不能交叉引用到 AI/ML/LLM 类别？

**能**。我已经做了交叉引用：

- `SimVecVis` 的**主条目**放在 `## 数据集与 Benchmark Suites` → `### 2025`，并加了锚点 `#simvecvis`
- 在 `### 文本/多模态可视化` 和 `### 评测方法学与复现` 里各放了一行 `*(cross-ref)*`，`Paper` 列用 `[见主条目](#simvecvis)` 跳转过去

这样就实现了你说的“它同时属于 dataset/benchmark，也属于 MLLM/LLM 方向”的**多归属**，同时避免同一条目复制粘贴多份难维护。

`README.md` 里原先还是 bullet 占位的这些部分，我都改成了和你现在“数据集与 Benchmark Suites”一致的**表格模板**（并保留占位行，方便你直接往里填）：

  - 可视化基础与设计理论、可视分析与交互、可解释性与模型可视化、图可视化、时序、高维、不确定性、SciVis、Geo…

---

### 你问的关键问题：同一篇论文在多个类别出现，怎么“确定是同一篇”，并且能跳转？

**有的**。我在 `阅读方式与条目格式` 里新增了“**唯一 ID 与交叉引用**”规范，核心就是：

- **主条目（唯一一处写全）**：在最合适的类别里写完整信息，并在前面加锚点

```markdown
<a id="paper-id"></a>
| 2025 | arXiv | Title | ... |
```

- **其他类别只放 cross-ref**：不要把整行再写一遍，而是用链接指向主条目

```markdown
| 2025 | arXiv | Title *(cross-ref)* | [见主条目](#paper-id) | - | - | tags |
```

这样读者一眼能看出：**这是同一篇论文**，并且点击就能跳到“唯一主条目”。

你现在的 `SimVecVis` 就已经按这个结构在 README 里实现了（主条目在数据集处，其他两处用 `#simvecvis` 跳转）。

如果你愿意，我下一步还能把这个“paper-id”的命名规则进一步标准化成你喜欢的格式（例如 `year-venue-firstauthor-shorttitle`），并同步更新到 `CONTRIBUTING.md`，让所有贡献者都按同一套来写。

### 你现在这份 `README.md` 里就是这么工作的
以 `SimVecVis` 为例：
- **主条目**上方我放了锚点：`<a id="simvecvis"></a>`
- 其他类别里的那一行在 `Paper`（或 Link）列写：`[见主条目](#simvecvis)`

所以你在交叉引用行里点击 **“见主条目”**，就会跳转到主条目所在位置。

@/Users/yutiaoyi/.cursor/projects/Users-yutiaoyi-developer-awesome-visualization/terminals/2.txt:12-19 
发生了什么东西？

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining git error steps**

I need to explain a git error in Chinese, focusing on key steps: starting with `git init`, then `add`, and committing before pushing. If there's no repository yet, they may have run `git remote add origin` incorrectly. The error message about the "src refspec main" indicates there’s no "main" branch or commits. I should include commands they need to use, like `git init`, `git commit`, `git branch -M main`, and `git push`. I'll keep it minimal.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

这是 Git 的一个典型报错：**你本地还没有任何可推送的 `main` 分支提交**。

### 具体发生了什么
- `git push -u origin main` 需要把本地的 `main` 分支推到远端。
- 但你当前仓库里 **还没有产生任何 commit**（或者甚至还没 `git init` 成功创建出有提交的分支），所以 Git 找不到可用的 `main` 引用，于是报：

- **`error: src refspec main does not match any`**：本地不存在名为 `main` 的有效引用（通常是“没有提交导致分支还没真正生成”）。

### 你该怎么修
在仓库根目录依次执行（有一步没做过就做哪一步）：

```bash
git init
git add .
git commit -m "init: awesome-visualization scaffold"
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

如果你已经 `git init` 过了，也已经 `remote add origin` 过了，上面照做也没问题（最多提示 remote 已存在）。如果你愿意，把你执行 `git status` 的输出贴出来，我可以精确告诉你当前卡在哪一步。
